method: maker≠checker — fresh project-factory reviewers over the staged working-tree diff (git diff --cached); the implementer did not review
evalJudge: not run — no eval case exists for this slice (numeric ledger queries are deterministic; see eval-decision.md)
verdict: clean — code reviewer APPROVE (no blockers/majors), security reviewer PASS (no findings), spec-compliance 9/9 scenarios implemented; all confirmed actionable findings fixed or accepted-with-rationale

specCompliance.note: Spec delta uses ## MODIFIED Requirements against the backfilled ledger baseline; FR-ACCT-02 per-account balance now closed via ledger queries.

resolvedFindings.summary: Postgres listNonDeleted ORDER BY created_at has no tiebreaker, so the first-seen group order in computeAccountBalances/computeCategoryTotals is non-deterministic when a bulk import shares one now() timestamp.
resolvedFindings.resolution: Changed to ORDER BY created_at, id in src/db/postgres.ts so group order is deterministic and matches the documented first-seen contract.

resolvedFindings.summary: The accounts page awaited the balance read together with the account list, so a thrown ledger read (e.g. a corrupt amount_minor) would break the whole account-management screen.
resolvedFindings.resolution: Isolated the balance read in app/accounts/page.tsx: the account list is fetched first, the balance read is wrapped in try/catch, and on failure each account shows a calm 'Баланс недоступний' indicator instead of a blank/500 page (FR-SHELL-03 explicit error state).

resolvedFindings.summary: formatUahMinor comment said 'uk-UA style' while using a plain ASCII space.
resolvedFindings.resolution: Reworded the comment in src/domain/money.ts to state the ASCII space is chosen for deterministic, locale-independent output (not the uk-UA non-breaking space).

resolvedFindings.summary: At audit time no reviews/ directory or review-findings.json existed, while the trajectory-eval waiver referenced review evidence.
resolvedFindings.resolution: Timing artifact: the auditor ran before the review step persisted its outputs. Raw reviewer outputs are now under reviews/ and linked from rawEvidence below; this review-findings.json is the summary. The deterministic check-trajectory will see clean review evidence after archive.

resolvedFindings.summary: Category-totals behavior was tested but not annotated @trace FR-CAT-04, leaving a trace test-coverage warning.
resolvedFindings.resolution: Added @trace FR-CAT-04 to the category-totals tests in ledger-query.test.ts and service.test.ts; the FR-CAT-04 test-trace warning is gone. FR-CAT-04 is not added to this slice's Refs (its UI ownership stays with ledger-items/dashboard); the tests only record that the enabling computation is exercised.

resolvedFindings.summary: tasks.md checkboxes were all unchecked despite the work being complete.
resolvedFindings.resolution: Ticked all completed task checkboxes in the change tasks.md.

acceptedFindings.summary: LedgerQueryService re-reads the full non-deleted table on every method (one listNonDeleted per call).
acceptedFindings.rationale: Accepted for v1 (documented as design D1). The accounts page makes a single call, so there is no current N-scan defect. The LedgerQueryPort shape lets a future dashboard slice add a one-pass fold without changing callers. No release-blocking impact.

securityFindings: none — security reviewer PASS: no injection (no interpolation/user input in listNonDeleted), no sensitive data exposure beyond intended balances, server-only DB boundary preserved (TC-STACK-04), no unhandled-500 path on empty/missing data, no new dependency or secret.
